name: Signal Event Rate Expansion Inventory
description: Declarative refinement inventory for signal to event to rate pipelines, covering pre-filter cleanup, signal-quality gating, and post-detection interval rejection.

operations.name: Insert Jump Removal Before Filter
operations.intent: Protect downstream detection from large waveform steps by inserting a waveform-preserving cleanup stage before the first filter.
operations.dejargonized_summary: Add a step that removes big jumps before filtering the signal.

rewrite.before_summary: A waveform source feeds the first detection-oriented filter directly.
rewrite.after_summary: A jump-removal stage is inserted between the waveform source and the first detection-oriented filter.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Preserves waveform semantics while removing large discontinuities ahead of downstream filtering.
notes: This rewrite is only appropriate while the pipeline still has access to the raw waveform path.

uncertainty_notes: This expansion is only safe when the pre-filter stage still has access to the waveform.

operations.name: Insert Signal Quality Gate Before Filter
operations.intent: Expose material waveform quality variation before the main filter so downstream detection does not commit to one brittle conditioning path.
operations.dejargonized_summary: Add a step that scores signal quality before the main filter when the waveform quality changes a lot over time.

rewrite.before_summary: A waveform source feeds the first detection-oriented filter without an explicit quality-assessment stage.
rewrite.after_summary: A signal-quality assessment stage is inserted before the first detection-oriented filter.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Adds a quality mask while preserving the waveform path into downstream filtering.
notes: This rewrite assumes the family still exposes a waveform path before filtering.

uncertainty_notes: This expansion assumes the family still exposes a waveform path before filtering.

operations.name: Insert Peak Correction After Detection
operations.intent: Refine detected event locations against the conditioned waveform before rate estimation when the event timing is only moderately inconsistent.
operations.dejargonized_summary: Add a step that nudges detected peaks onto better waveform locations before computing the final rate.

rewrite.before_summary: A detector feeds a rate estimator directly, with no explicit event-timing refinement stage.
rewrite.after_summary: A peak-correction stage consumes both the conditioned waveform and the detected events before the rate estimator.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Preserves the waveform path long enough to correct event timing before the final lossy rate conversion.
notes: This refinement depends on the filtered waveform still being available alongside the detected events.

uncertainty_notes: This refinement is most appropriate when the detector is mostly right but the event timing is still locally unstable.

operations.name: Insert Outlier Rejection After Detection
operations.intent: Protect rate estimation from an implausible event stream by inserting interval-based event rejection after detection.
operations.dejargonized_summary: Add a cleanup step between peak detection and rate calculation when the detected timing is obviously inconsistent.

rewrite.before_summary: A detector feeds an event-rate estimator directly.
rewrite.after_summary: An interval-rejection stage is inserted between detection and the event-rate estimator.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Removes implausible events before rate estimation while keeping the event stream interpretation explicit.
notes: This refinement is intentionally lossy and should only fire after event extraction.

uncertainty_notes: This refinement is lossy and should only fire after event extraction.

operations.name: Insert Outlier Rejection Before Robust Smoothed Rate
operations.intent: Respond to unstable event spacing by cleaning the event stream and replacing the final measure stage with a more robust smoothed rate estimator.
operations.dejargonized_summary: When detected event timing is unstable, clean the events and use a more robust smoothed rate calculator instead of the raw rate step.

rewrite.before_summary: A detector feeds a raw event-rate estimator directly.
rewrite.after_summary: An interval-rejection stage is inserted and the final rate stage is replaced with a more robust smoothed estimator.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Cleans unstable events before the final lossy rate conversion and makes the final measure stage less sensitive to local jitter.
notes: This refinement is intentionally lossy and should only fire after event extraction.

uncertainty_notes: This refinement changes the final measure stage, so it should only fire when event-spacing instability is materially present.

operations.name: Insert Outlier Rejection Before Smoothed Rate
operations.intent: Apply the same event-stream cleanup before the smoothed rate estimator when detected intervals are implausible.
operations.dejargonized_summary: Add the same event cleanup step before the smoothed rate calculator when the detected timing is inconsistent.

rewrite.before_summary: A detector feeds the smoothed event-rate estimator directly.
rewrite.after_summary: An interval-rejection stage is inserted between detection and the smoothed event-rate estimator.
rewrite.information_flow_effect: Applies the same event cleanup before the smoothed rate estimator path.
notes: This refinement is intentionally lossy and should only fire after event extraction.

uncertainty_notes: This refinement is lossy and should only fire after event extraction.

audit.rationale: Externalizes high-value signal-event-rate refinements that previously lived only in framework-local rule code.

migration_readiness.rationale: The refinement inventory is now declarative and reviewable, but it still depends on legacy runtime matching in this repository.

checklist.description: The expansion asset schema is declarative and loads without custom parsing.
evidence: The family asset validates through the shared expansion asset model.

checklist.description: Each operation has intent, rewrite rationale, and dejargonized documentation.
evidence: All operations include intent, rewrite summaries, and uncertainty notes.

checklist.description: The asset no longer relies on legacy topology-only runtime matching.
notes: Boundary-aware applicability still requires framework-local compatibility logic.

audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the family-level cleanups we know can help a waveform-to-rate pipeline when the signal is jumpy, the quality varies, or the detected events are implausible.
uncertainty_notes: The current runtime still adapts these assets through the legacy rewrite engine.
Boundary-aware applicability remains limited by the current CDG topology representation.

references.title: Phase 3 Expansion Asset Plan
references.note: Framework plan for promoting refinement inventories into auditable family assets.
